name: Neighborhood Housing Services Inc Dba Neighborworks Boise
description_short: NeighborWorks Boise is a nonprofit mortgage lender and affordable housing developer serving Idaho with homebuyer education, down payment assistance, and home loans for underserved populations.
description_long:
NeighborWorks Boise is a nonprofit organization established to create affordable housing opportunities in the Boise and Treasure Valley regions of Idaho. Founded with a mission to develop innovative housing solutions for underserved populations through strategic partnerships, the organization operates as both a lender and a housing developer committed to strengthening communities through homeownership and rental housing.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of homeownership programs including HUD-certified homebuyer education and financial coaching, down payment assistance grants, and home loans with flexible underwriting criteria designed to serve borrowers who may not qualify through traditional lenders. They also develop and sell new construction affordable homes (such as Cole Bluff Cottages), manage rental apartment communities for families and seniors, and offer the Pocket Neighborhoods program—compact single-family homes designed to be more affordable while maintaining quality construction.

NeighborWorks Boise differentiates itself through its integration of community service with lending and housing development. Beyond mortgages, they operate Paint the Town (established 1982, serving 3,400+ households), which provides volunteer home painting for disabled, senior, and veteran neighbors, and Rake Up Boise (since 1985), a yard maintenance program. All homes feature green building practices including energy conservation, water efficiency, clean air systems, and healthy construction materials.

As a nonprofit focused on underserved populations, NeighborWorks Boise serves an important market segment but operates with geographic limitations to Idaho and appears to have lending volume constraints typical of mission-driven organizations. Borrowers should confirm current loan program availability and underwriting standards, as nonprofit community lenders often have more limited capacity and specialized requirements than conventional mortgage lenders.
pros: FREE financial coaching provided by HUD-certified counselors
Down payment assistance grants available to reduce upfront costs
Homebuyer education program with flexible lending options to decrease monthly payments
New construction homes built to green building standards with energy and water conservation features
Integrated community programs (Paint the Town, Rake Up Boise) demonstrating organizational values
Affordable rental apartments available for families, seniors, and individuals
Pocket Neighborhoods program offers compact, affordable single-family homes in existing neighborhoods
cons: Geographic service area limited to Boise and Treasure Valley regions of Idaho
Website displays placeholder metrics (homes built, loans issued all showing '0'), suggesting limited loan volume or recent website updates
No specific information provided about loan terms, interest rates, or underwriting criteria
Limited staffing transparency—'Our Team' page content incomplete on provided website excerpt
As a nonprofit, likely has more restrictive lending capacity and longer processing timelines than conventional lenders
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Boise/Treasure Valley region who need education and down payment assistance
Underserved borrowers (low-to-moderate income) who may not qualify for conventional mortgages
Buyers interested in affordable new construction homes with green building features
Low-income renters seeking affordable apartment communities in Idaho
diagnosis: NeighborWorks Boise is best for first-time homebuyers and underserved populations in the Boise area seeking affordable mortgages paired with financial coaching and down payment help. Primary caveat: as a nonprofit with likely limited lending capacity and geographic scope restricted to Idaho, this lender may have longer timelines, more rigid program requirements, and smaller loan volumes than mainstream mortgage providers.
services: Home purchase mortgages with flexible underwriting
FREE financial coaching with HUD-certified counselors
Down payment assistance grants and programs
New construction affordable home sales (Cole Bluff Cottages, Pocket Neighborhoods)
Home repair programs (Meridian Repair Program, NWB Home Repair Program)
Green building design and construction practices
Community volunteer programs (Paint the Town, Rake Up Boise)
Homes for sale listings and real estate services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3380 W Americana Ter Ste 120, Boise, ID 83706
